user_query:
I’m producing a conversational podcast episode about a single book and need a fully fleshed-out two-person dialogue script. Please read the following inputs:
- input/book.json (book metadata: title and author)
- input/focus.md (themes/chapters to emphasize)
- input/personas.yaml (brief traits for the host and the guest expert)

Then create a 10–15 minute, 2,500–3,000 word Markdown script that turns the book into a natural, engaging conversation between a host and a guest expert. Follow these requirements:
1) Structure and formatting:
- Use the exact section headers: "## [OPENING]", "## [TOPIC 1]", "## [TOPIC 2]", "## [TOPIC 3]" (and optionally "## [TOPIC 4]" if needed), "## [RAPID FIRE]", "## [KEY TAKEAWAYS]", and "## [CLOSING]".
- Begin with a top-level title line as a Markdown H1 that includes the book title (e.g., "# [Book Title] — Dialogue Podcast Script"). Include two lines near the top with the format "**Author**: ..." and "**Duration**: ~X minutes".
- Use bold speaker labels exactly as "**Host**:" and "**Guest**:" for every line of dialogue.

2) Conversation style:
- Keep exchanges short and dynamic with natural markers like "[laughs]", "[pause]", or "[thinking]" sprinkled appropriately.
- Include at least one moment where the host challenges/pushes back on an idea and the guest replies with a "well, actually" clarification.
- Include at least one moment of genuine surprise, phrased as "I never thought of it that way" by either speaker.
- Each TOPIC section should have multiple back-and-forth exchanges (not monologues) and should meaningfully incorporate the focus areas from input/focus.md.
- The RAPID FIRE section should contain at least three quick Q&As.
- In KEY TAKEAWAYS, have each speaker share exactly one #1 takeaway.

3) Language and tone:
- Write in English only.
- Make it feel like a real conversation: occasional overlaps or brief interruptions are fine; be clear and engaging.
- If the book is obscure or uncertain, be transparent rather than fabricating specifics.

Deliverables:
- Write the complete script to output/script.md
- Also write a one-paragraph show description to output/description.txt

Please proceed after reading the inputs and produce both output files under output/.
